title: Engaging with Music
catalogDescription: Helps students understand their experiences making music in its broadest social and historical contexts. Includes hands-on projects involving personal, local, and university histories with music, as well as current events. Offered: W.

title: Introduction to the History of Western Music I
catalogDescription: Introduction to the critical study of Western music history, including representative composers, works, and genres, as well as significant concepts and issues, antiquity to 1670. Prerequisite: MUHST 100; and MUSIC 203 or MUSIC 206, either of which may be taken concurrently. Offered: A.
prerequisiteNotes: Official UW prerequisite text: MUHST 100; and MUSIC 203 or MUSIC 206, either of which may be taken concurrently.

title: Introduction to the History of Western Music II
catalogDescription: Introduction to the critical study of Western music history, including representative composers, works, and genres, as well as significant concepts and issues. Baroque and classical periods. Prerequisite: a minimum grade of 2.0 in MUHST 210; a minimum grade of 2.0 in MUSIC 203; and a minimum grade of 2.0 in MUSIC 206. Offered: W.

title: Introduction to the History of Western Music III
catalogDescription: Introduction to the critical study of Western music history including representative composers, works, and genres as well as significant concepts and issues - nineteenth and twentieth centuries. Prerequisite: minimum grade of 2.0 in MUHST 211. Offered: Sp.

title: Medieval Music: To 1400
catalogDescription: Critical readings on issues in medieval music. Works to be studied include repertory from chant, motets, and sacred and secular music of the Middle Ages.

title: Early British Music: 1300-1700
catalogDescription: Examines the history of British music from its earliest polyphony through the music of Purcell. Stylistic features of English music studied, including medieval polyphony, Tudor music, Elizabethan music, and seventeenth-century music through Purcell.

title: Baroque Choral Music
catalogDescription: Monteverdi through Handel. Prerequisite: minimum grade of 2.0 in each of MUHST 210, MUSIC 303, and MUSIC 306.

title: Wagner's "Ring" Cycle
catalogDescription: Wagner's Der Ring des Nibelungen, combining musical analysis of the four music dramas with a study of their mythological, political, intellectual, and philosophical context. Prerequisite: a minimum grade of 2.0 in MUHST 210; a minimum grade of 2.0 in MUSIC 302; and a minimum grade of 2.0 in MUSIC 305.

title: Jazz History and Analysis
catalogDescription: Major eras and styles of jazz with emphasis on technical aspects of jazz music: composition, arranging, improvisation practices. Prerequisite: minimum grade of 2.0 in each of MUHST 210, MUSIC 303, and MUSIC 306.

title: Seminar in Methods of Music Research
catalogDescription: Explores various critical approaches to research in music at the graduate level, examining specialized bibliographical resources, controversial arguments about musical issues, and other matters of musical criticism required to begin advanced study of music. Prerequisite for all graduate music history courses except MUHST 515.

title: Music History Colloquium
catalogDescription: Research forum in music history with presentations by students, faculty, and invited speakers. Discussion of contemporary methods and issues in the field. Professionalization workshops centering on pedagogy, on conference participation and peer-reviewed publication, and on applications (e.g., for grants, jobs, and further study). Offered: AWSp.
